title: Crepidomanes majoriae
taxonomy: Crepidomanes majoriae is classified within the family Hymenophyllaceae, which is a family of ferns commonly known as the filmy ferns. This family is part of the larger division Pteridophyta, which includes all vascular plants that produce spores instead of seeds. Crepidomanes majoriae is specifically placed within the genus Crepidomanes, a genus that contains over 200 species of filmy ferns. The genus Crepidomanes is further classified within the order Hymenophyllales, which is a distinct order of ferns characterized by their delicate, filigree-like fronds. The classification of Crepidomanes majoriae reflects its unique characteristics and relationships to other species of filmy ferns.

intro: Crepidomanes majoriae is a species of fern, specifically a type of epiphytic fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This plant is valued for its traditional medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress.
